name: Safra Catz
bio: Safra Catz has served as CEO of Oracle since 2014, overseeing one of the world's largest enterprise software companies. She is known for her financial acumen and has driven Oracle's aggressive cloud transformation strategy.

name: Fei-Fei Li
bio: Fei-Fei Li is a leading AI researcher and Professor at Stanford University. She co-founded AI4ALL to increase diversity in AI and previously served as Chief Scientist of AI/ML at Google Cloud.

name: Reshma Saujani
bio: Reshma Saujani founded Girls Who Code in 2012 to close the gender gap in technology. The organisation has reached over 500,000 girls across the US and 6 other countries with coding and tech education programs.

name: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw
bio: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw founded Biocon in 1978 and built it into India's largest biopharmaceutical company. She is a pioneer in affordable biosimilars and has been instrumental in making biologics accessible in emerging markets.

name: Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala
bio: Dr. Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala became the first woman and first African to lead the WTO in 2021. She has championed digital trade frameworks and equitable access to health technologies for developing nations.

name: Anne Wojcicki
bio: Anne Wojcicki co-founded 23andMe in 2006, pioneering direct-to-consumer genetic testing. She has driven the democratisation of genomic data and its application to personalised healthcare and drug discovery.

name: Juliana Rotich
bio: Juliana Rotich is a Kenyan technologist and co-founder of Ushahidi, a globally recognised open-source crisis mapping platform. She serves on multiple boards and champions digital infrastructure development across Africa.

name: Padmasree Warrior
bio: Padmasree Warrior is former CTO of Cisco and Motorola and founder of Fable, an interactive storytelling platform. She is recognised as one of the most powerful women in technology globally.

name: Ory Okolloh
bio: Ory Okolloh is a Kenyan lawyer, activist and technologist who co-founded Ushahidi. She previously led Google's Policy and Government Relations for Africa and is a prominent voice on digital rights and open governance.

name: Mariana Mazzucato
bio: Mariana Mazzucato is a world-renowned economist and founder of the UCL Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose. She advises governments globally on mission-driven innovation and has shaped digital health investment policy at WHO.

name: Neha Narkhede
bio: Neha Narkhede co-founded Confluent and was the original creator of Apache Kafka at LinkedIn. She is now CEO of Oscilar, building AI-powered risk decisioning platforms for financial institutions.

name: Radhika Aggarwal
bio: Radhika Aggarwal co-founded ShopClues, one of India's leading online marketplaces. She has been instrumental in bringing tier-2 and tier-3 city merchants online and democratising e-commerce across India.

name: Shafi Goldwasser
bio: Shafi Goldwasser is a two-time Turing Award winner and pioneer in cryptography. Her work on probabilistic encryption and zero-knowledge proofs forms the foundation of modern internet security.

name: Nora Denzel
bio: Nora Denzel serves on the boards of AMD and other major tech companies. She is a champion of diversity in tech and has held senior leadership roles at Intuit, HP and Novell throughout her career.

name: Alicia Framis
bio: Alicia Framis works at the intersection of art, technology and society, exploring how AI shapes human relationships. She leads research initiatives on ethical AI design and digital identity across European institutions.
